tools.title: Bitcoin Tools
tools.subtitle: Powerful tools to help you understand Bitcoin markets, plan your investment strategy, and validate addresses.
tools.priceConverterDesc: Convert between Bitcoin, satoshi, and major fiat currencies (USD, EUR, JPY, GBP, CNY, CHF, INR, BRL) using real-time exchange rates.
tools.marketDataDesc: Real-time Bitcoin price data, market statistics, halving countdown, price charts, and transaction fee estimates.
tools.dcaSimulatorDesc: Compare Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA) vs Lump Sum investment strategies with historical and projected performance analysis.
tools.bip39SeedDesc: Generate or manually create BIP39 mnemonic seed phrases (12 or 24 words) with autocomplete validation from the official wordlist.
tools.addressValidatorDesc: Validate Bitcoin addresses and identify their type: Legacy (1...), SegWit (3...), or Native SegWit (bc1...).
tools.btcTreasuriesDesc: View the Top 20 Bitcoin treasury public companies.
tools.securityWarningDesc: When using Bitcoin tools, always follow these security best practices:
tools.securityTip1: Always verify Bitcoin addresses before sending funds - double-check characters, especially at the beginning and end of the address
tools.securityTip2: Never share your private keys, seed phrases, or wallet passwords with anyone
tools.securityTip3: Use trusted, open-source software and verify downloads from official sources
tools.securityTip4: Be aware that some tools are for educational purposes only - use production-grade tools for real transactions
tools.securityTip5: Consider using hardware wallets (cold wallets) for storing significant amounts of Bitcoin
tools.securityTip6: Keep your software and wallets updated with the latest security patches
tools.securityTip7: Double-check transaction amounts and recipient addresses before confirming

addressValidator.title: Bitcoin Address Validator
addressValidator.subtitle: Validate Bitcoin addresses and identify their type
addressValidator.label: Bitcoin Address
addressValidator.securityWarningDesc: When working with Bitcoin addresses, always follow these security best practices:
addressValidator.securityTip1: Always verify addresses before sending funds - double-check specially characters in the beginning and end of the address
addressValidator.securityTip2: Be aware that bech32 addresses (starting with bc1 or tb1) are case-sensitive
addressValidator.securityTip3: Watch out for typos or address manipulation attacks - malicious software can swap addresses
addressValidator.securityTip4: Never share private keys or seed phrases with anyone
addressValidator.securityTip5: Consider using cold wallets for generating and managing addresses securely
addressValidator.securityTip6: Understand that address reuse can compromise privacy - use new addresses for each transaction when possible
addressValidator.typeP2PKH: Pay-to-Public-Key-Hash (P2PKH) address. The original Bitcoin address format.
addressValidator.typeP2SH: Pay-to-Script-Hash (P2SH) address. Supports Segregated Witness (SegWit) wrapped in P2SH.
addressValidator.typeP2WPKH: Pay-to-Witness-Public-Key-Hash (P2WPKH) address. Native Segregated Witness format with lower transaction fees.
addressValidator.typeP2WSH: Pay-to-Witness-Script-Hash (P2WSH) address. Native SegWit script format.
addressValidator.typeP2TR: Pay-to-Taproot (P2TR) address. The newest and most efficient Bitcoin address format with enhanced privacy.

bip39Seed.securityTip1: Generate seed phrases offline using trusted software
